leadText: Infernape (Japanese: ゴウカザル Goukazaru) is a dual-type Fire/Fighting Pokémon introduced in Generation IV. It evolves from Monferno starting at level 36. It is the final form of Chimchar.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Infernape is a bipedal, primate-like Pokémon that is primarily reddish-brown with sections of white fur on its chest, head, and lower legs. Several gold markings adorn its body: swirled, circular ones on its knees and shoulders, flame-shaped ones on the back of its hands, and a stripe around its back that forms two swirls on its chest. On top of its head is a large flame, which is said to never go out. Infernape has an orange, rounded muzzle, long, round ears with blue inside, and a red ridge over its eyes. The eyes themselves are blue with yellow sclerae. There are five blue digits on both its feet and hands, and it has a long tail. This agile Pokémon practices a unique kind of martial art that involves all of its limbs, as it shrouds itself in flame and uses fiery punches and kicks. This form of battle is comparable to dancing. Infernape is also known to overwhelm opponents with its unrivalled speed during combat. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Infernape evolves from Monferno and is the final form of Chimchar . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Chimchar Fire → First Evolution Monferno Fire Fighting → Second Evolution Infernape Fire Fighting

This Chimchar will eventually evolve into Infernape. Additionally, in the Battle Tower in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, he may use an Infernape during a Double Battle with Palmer against the player, regardless of which Pokémon was chosen at the beginning of the game. In Pokémon Diamond, Pearl and Platinum, as well as Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, Flint will use an Infernape as part of his team. Additionally, in the Battle Tower in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl, he may use an Infernape during a Double Battle with Volkner against the player. In Pokémon Diamond and Pearl , during a Multi Battle challenge in the Battle Tower, one of the Pokémon that Riley can select to help the player is an Infernape. In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 , during the Sinnoh Leaders Tournament of the Pokémon World Tournament , Maylene may use an Infernape as part of her team. Additionally, in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , Maylene will use an Infernape in a post-game rematch against her, and can also use one during Master Class Single Battles in the Battle Tower. Either Lucas or Dawn will use an Infernape, evolved from their Chimchar, in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl during post game battles against them, if the player chose Piplup . In Pokémon Scarlet and Violet : The Indigo Disk , after Mochi Mayhem has been cleared, Nemona will use an Infernape as part of her team, if the player chose Fuecoco . In the side series games [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Battle Revolution , during the Masters Battle Set 1, Joe will use an Infernape as part of his team. Its fire never goes out. Pearl Its crown of fire is indicative of its fiery nature. It is beaten by none in terms of quickness. Platinum It uses unique fighting moves with fire on its hands and feet. It will take on any opponent. HeartGold It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style. sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry DP163 Infernape Dawn's Pokédex Infernape, the Flame Pokémon and the evolved form of Monferno. Infernape's fiery crown reflects its fiery nature, and its speed is unmatched.
